specification.topic-statement: The basic REPL commands provide conversation reset, command listing, and application exit as core built-in operations available in every agent session.

scope.in-scope: The /clear, /help, and /exit commands: their argument handling, the sequence of side effects each produces, and their error conditions.
scope.boundary: How system prompt events are formatted and appended to the scrollback is covered by the event rendering specification.
Log file rotation mechanics are covered by the debug log file specification.
How slash commands are parsed, matched by name, and routed to handlers is covered by the command dispatch specification.

behavior.clear-command:
The `/clear` command resets the active agent's conversation state to the same condition it would be in at the start of a fresh session.
The debug log file is rotated: the current log is closed and a new log file is opened in the current working directory. If the current working directory cannot be determined, the command returns an IO error without performing any further steps.
The following in-memory state is discarded in order: - The scrollback display buffer is cleared of all visible lines. - All messages in the agent's conversation array are freed and the count is reset to zero. - The token cache, if present, is reset (all turn counts cleared). - All conversation marks are freed and the mark count is reset to zero. - Any active autocomplete state is cleared.
If a database connection and session identifier are present: - A boundary record of kind "clear" with no content is inserted into the messages table. - The agent's effective system prompt is resolved and, if obtained, a record of kind "system" containing that prompt is inserted. Database failures at either step are logged as warnings and do not prevent the command from succeeding; in-memory state is authoritative.
The agent's effective system prompt is resolved again. If obtained, it is rendered as a system event into the now-empty scrollback. A failure to render to the scrollback is the only error condition that causes the clear command to return an error.
The effective system prompt is resolved once for database persistence and a second time for scrollback rendering. The two calls are independent; each reads from the pinned document list, filesystem, and configuration in turn.
behavior.help-command:
The `/help` command lists all registered slash commands in alphabetical order. Any arguments provided are ignored.
A header line "Available commands:" is appended to the scrollback. The command registry is then retrieved and the entries are sorted alphabetically by name using a stable index-based sort. For each entry in sorted order, a line formatted as " / - " is appended to the scrollback. Errors from any scrollback append operation are returned immediately.
behavior.exit-command:
The `/exit` command signals the event loop to terminate and cancels all active LLM requests. Any arguments provided are ignored.
All agents in the REPL's agent array have their provider instances invalidated, aborting any in-flight HTTP connections. A quit flag on the REPL context is then set. The event loop checks this flag at the top of each iteration and exits when it is set. The exit command always returns success immediately; the process does not terminate until the event loop exits naturally.
